baseline_description: Use ONLY for comparative research that informs a concrete decision through multi-candidate comparison, literature review, and a multi-file paper-style report. NOT for single-source lookups (use librarian), quick fact-checks, or library docs queries.

candidates.description: Use for comparative research that helps the user choose among multiple candidates, shortlist options, or produce a recommendation using explicit criteria and a paper-style findings package. Lean toward triggering when the prompt asks to compare, evaluate options, pick a tool/platform/library/vendor, or recommend one path from several viable alternatives. NOT for single-source lookups or quick fact-checks (use librarian), library docs queries (use librarian), implementation tasks, diagnosis, or architecture judgement (use architect-review).

Use when a user needs decision-informed comparative research: multi-candidate comparison, option scoring, shortlist creation, literature review, and a recommendation report. Trigger on genuine comparison requests even if phrased casually, such as choosing between tools, platforms, libraries, vendors, or stacks. NOT for single-source lookups or quick fact-checks (use librarian), library docs queries (use librarian), code implementation, debugging, or architecture judgement (use architect-review).

Use for multi-candidate comparison that must inform a concrete recommendation, especially when the user asks for a shortlist, evaluation matrix, tradeoff analysis, or paper-style report. Prefer triggering for substantial choose-between-options prompts with criteria, constraints, or stakeholders; the skill can help structure the comparative research before recommending. NOT for single-source lookups or quick fact-checks (use librarian), library docs queries (use librarian), implementation tasks, diagnosis, teaching-only deep dives, or architecture judgement (use architect-review).

best_description: Use when a user needs decision-informed comparative research: multi-candidate comparison, option scoring, shortlist creation, literature review, and a recommendation report. Trigger on genuine comparison requests even if phrased casually, such as choosing between tools, platforms, libraries, vendors, or stacks. NOT for single-source lookups or quick fact-checks (use librarian), library docs queries (use librarian), code implementation, debugging, or architecture judgement (use architect-review).
